resources.string: Do I have to sign up?
Nope. No login, no email, no password to remember. Just open the app and go.
Do I always need internet?
Only to browse the catalog and download stuff. Once a mod is on your phone, you can play offline (as long as Minecraft itself works offline).
Which Minecraft versions work?
Android Minecraft Bedrock — the latest version and the few before it. Every mod tells you which exact versions it\'s for, look near the file.
How do I install a mod?
Open the mod, pick a file from the list, hit download, then install. Minecraft will pop up and grab it on its own. Last step — turn the pack on inside the world you want to play.
What about texture packs and skins?
Same flow as mods. After installing, switch them on inside Minecraft — textures live under Global Resources, skins under your Profile.
Why doesn\'t the mod install?

Quick checks first:
• The mod must match your Minecraft version — see the version line on the mod page.
• Your phone needs free space.
• Close Minecraft and reopen it (this one fixes it surprisingly often).
Still nothing? Download the file again and retry.
I got a \"file corrupted\" error
Means the download cut off halfway. Delete what you have, grab it again on a stable connection (Wi-Fi if you can) and you should be good.
I installed the mod but I don\'t see anything in the game
The mod is in Minecraft, you just have to switch it on. Open your world\'s settings and enable the pack. Some mods also need Experimental Features turned on — same settings menu.
How do I turn on Experimental Features?

When you create or edit a world, scroll down to Experiments and flip on the toggle your mod needs (the mod usually tells you which one — often \"Holiday Creator Features\" or \"Beta APIs\"). Reload the world after.
The game lags after I installed a mod

Some mods are just heavy. Things to try, in order:
• Close other apps running in the background.
• Lower the render distance in Minecraft (this one helps the most).
• Drop graphics quality a notch.
If it's still rough, the mod might be too big for the device — try a lighter one.
Can I use mods on a server with friends?
Depends on the server. Usually every player needs the same packs installed — if the server you\'re joining allows the mod, you\'re fine. If it doesn\'t, the mod just won\'t do anything there.
Are you adding new mods?
All the time. Refresh the catalog and you\'ll see what showed up since last time.
How do I know if a mod got updated?
When the mod author ships a new version, we add it to the catalog. The version sits right next to the file on the mod page.
Are the mods safe?
Yeah. Every mod gets checked before it goes into the catalog — no viruses, no weird stuff hiding in the file.
Why are there ads?
Servers and updates aren\'t free, and ads are how we pay for them — that\'s how the app itself stays free for you. We try to keep them out of your way while you\'re actually using the app.
It says \"no internet\" but my internet works
If your connection is fine and the app still shouts about it, our servers are probably napping for a bit. Give it a few minutes and try again.
If I delete this app, do I lose my mods?
No. The mods live inside Minecraft now — they stay there even if our app goes.
Will mods mess up my worlds?
Not on their own. Mods are added as packs in Minecraft and you choose which world they go into. If you\'re trying a new mod and don\'t want to risk a world you care about — start a new world for it.
How do I delete a mod I don\'t want anymore?
Inside Minecraft, open Packs (Behavior, Resource, or Skin depending on the type), long-press the one you want gone, and pick Delete. Your worlds stay safe — only the mod is removed.
Where do I tell you something is broken?
Use the Feedback section on the Propose screen. Write what happened — the more detail you give us, the faster we can figure it out.
I made my own mod — can I send it to you?
Sure thing. Open the Propose screen and drop a link or attach the file there. We look through submissions and add the ones that fit.
